description:
Learn how to create effective and funder-compliant Data Management Plans (DMPs) in this hands-on workshop led by [UBC’s Research Data Management team](https://researchcommons.library.ubc.ca/the-team/), designed for graduate students and researchers across disciplines. Participants will explore best practices in research data planning and gain experience using the [DMP Assistant tool](https://dmp-pgd.ca/), a free, bilingual platform maintained by the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

This session uses UBC’s [Simplified DMP Template](https://ubc-library-rc.github.io/rdm/content/08_Simplified-Data-Management-Plan.html) to make data management planning accessible and practical.

By the end, participants will have:

- A clear understanding of the components of a good Data Management Plan

- Confidence navigating the DMP Assistant tool

- Insight into applying data management principles to their research projects

#### Location: ONLINE

(A Zoom link will be sent to registrants 3 hours before the event starts.)
name: Data Bites - Introduction to Data Management Plans (DMPs)

audience.audienceType: Institutional staff who interact with users or support user-facing systems

description:
Using language and methodologies from North American emergency services, this training establishes a common language, behaviors, and procedures for responding to cybersecurity incidents. Attendees will receive worksheets to guide them through the process.

Participants will evaluate and respond to a mock cybersecurity incident. The focus is on understanding when the process should begin, what information is helpful, and which behaviors and decisions lead to better outcomes. 

_An identical session will also be offered on April 27._

**About the Instructor**: Karl Vollmer has worked in critical IT systems roles for over 25 years, in addition to 10 years with Halifax Regional Fire and Emergency, rising to the rank of Station Captain, and 5 years with Halifax Ground Search and Rescue as part of its swift water rescue team. Holding nationally recognized advanced leadership certifications, Red Seal NFPA Fire Instructor I, and Blue Card Incident Command, Karl brings real-world emergency services training and experience to IT systems management and cybersecurity.
name: Cybersecurity Incident Response - Session 1

organizer.name: National Security Council (NSC) - Security Training and Awareness Group

description:
This six-week class will introduce neural network programming concepts, theory and techniques. The class material will begin at an introductory level, intended for those with no experience with neural networks, eventually covering intermediate-to-advanced concepts.
	Classes will be held, April 7 - May 14, 2026, Tuesdays and Thursdays, 11:00am - 12:00pm.
	Format: Virtual
name: Neural network programming: lecture 5

description:
[Register](https://forms.gle/Uu1o42F9k6Vh7oCz6)  
   
Abstract: Typst is a new open-source markup typesetting system that aims to be as powerful as LaTeX while offering a much faster compiler, simpler installation, fewer moving parts, and a gentler learning curve.  
The straightforward and unobtrusive syntax allows for math formulae, tables, graphs, bibliography management, and presentation mode.  
Scripting (conditionals, loops, functions, and methods) as well as data loading produce reactive documents with automatically updated tables or plots.  
Finally, Typst integrates perfectly with Quarto.  
While this tool is new and still in active development, it already has over 50k stars on GitHub and a contributors pool of over 400 people, making it a very popular tool embraced by the scientific community.  
If you are using a word processing program (e.g. MS Word) to write your thesis, articles, and other scientific documents, Typst can easily allow you to switch to a text-based approach with all the benefits that this entails (version control with Git, use of a powerful text editor, embedding code in documents, etc.)
name: Typst: a new markup typesetting system [webinar]

description:
Every year, the Canadian Research Data Centre Network supports the next generation of highly qualified personnel through the CRDCN Emerging Scholars Award. Award recipients are doctoral students at Canadian universities using Research Data Centre data to conduct their research.
The CRDCN Emerging Scholars Webinar will showcase their research and their pathway to the RDC. The April 2026 webinar will feature presentations by ten Emerging Scholars in both English and French.
Please join us to learn more about what students are doing in the RDCs!
name: Emerging Scholars Webinar - Spring 2026

alternateName: Aspects éthiques de l’utilisation de l’intelligence artificielle en recherche
description:
### **Intégrité 360° : pour une science qui inspire confiance**

Le Centre de recherche Azrieli du CHU Sainte-Justine vous invite à une **série de conférences sur la conduite responsable en recherche** qui se poursuit pendant l'année 2026. Ces conférences visent à explorer les enjeux essentiels de l’intégrité scientifique et à promouvoir des pratiques responsables qui renforcent la confiance dans la recherche.

Soyez des nôtres, en ligne ou en présentiel, pour la deuxième demi-journée de partage des connaissances, le **22 avril, 9h-13h**.

**22 avril : Aspects éthiques de l’utilisation de l’intelligence artificielle en recherche**

Présentation 1 : **Patientes et patients producteurs de données, IA et consentement - Dominique Leclerc**, Scénariste et réalisatrice du long métrage documentaire [*Posthumains*](https://www.onf.ca/film/posthumains/), autrice, co-metteure en scène et interprète de [*Une vie intelligente*](https://duceppe.com/une-vie-intelligente/)

Présentation 2 : **Intelligence artificielle et écriture scientifique : vers de nouvelles pratiques responsables - Martine Peters**, Directrice du [Partenariat universitaire sur la prévention du plagiat (PUPP)](https://pupp.uqo.ca/fr/accueil/), chercheuse et professeure en science de l’éducation à l’Université du Québec en Outaouais

Présentation 3 : **Utilisation de la science des données et l’IA pour optimiser la prise en charge en soins intensifs pédiatriques, un exemple d’intégration responsable - Dr Guillaume Emeriaud, M.D., Ph.D.,** Médecin intensiviste pédiatrique et clinicien-chercheur au CHU Sainte-Justine, professeur au département de pédiatrie de l’Université de Montréal

[**Réservez la date**](https://www.chusj.org/fr/Calendrier-salle-presse/calendrier/Evenements/CDR/2026/Integrite-360%C2%B0-pour-une-science-qui-inspire-co-\(1\)) **dès maintenant, consultez le [programme complet](https://www.chusj.org/fr/Calendrier-salle-presse/calendrier/Evenements/CDR/2026/Integrite-360%C2%B0-pour-une-science-qui-inspire-co-\(1\)) et** [**inscrivez-vous**](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=KP7hBotfdUC_bK4kvhp5klyDkgYzaPZEotMEqD6NN0lUQ0UwT05IQVdENDQ4VjJXVEFZMThKODQyUS4u) **!** La **traduction simultanée français-anglais** sera disponible en ligne.

**[Sessions à venir](https://enseignement.chusj.org/fr/bibliotheques/les-Ressources/Gestion-de-donnees-de-recherche/Evenements) :**

*   18 juin, 13h-17h : Science ouverte, transparence et reproductibilité des résultats
*   22 octobre, 9h-13h : Intégrité scientifique dans le processus de publication

Pour toute question, n’hésitez pas à nous contacter à **[soutien.gdr.hsj@ssss.gouv.qc.ca](mailto:soutien.gdr.hsj@ssss.gouv.qc.ca)**

Nous espérons vous compter parmi nous !

description:
This session will introduce participants to the philosophy of minimal computing with a 'show-and-tell' tour of a variety of static websites hosted on GitHub Pages. Use cases such as conference websites, digital exhibits, and open science will be explored. No prior experience with coding or GitHub is required.  

The format for the session will be a presentation followed by a discussion and question period. This session will be recorded; presentation slides and recording will be available to attendees. 

**About the speaker**

Sandra Sawchuk is the Research Data & Assessment Librarian at Mount Saint Vincent University Library & Archives in Halifax, NS. Her research includes the digital and spatial humanities, data rescue and reuse, and computational reproducibility. She was recently awarded a SSHRC Connection Grant for the event 'Research Data Management as a Service: Connecting Research Data Support in Atlantic Canada', which aims to bring regional RDM practitioners together to co-create and share best practices, resources, and training materials.
name: Hosting a Project Website on GitHub: Introduction to Minimal Computing and Static Websites

organizer.name: [NoE] Curation Events Working Group (CEWG): Curation Clinic

description:
Presenters: Tyson Whitehead (SHARCNET)
 
Simple Features is a standardized (ISO 19125-1:2004) architecture for geographic information (points, lines, and polygons). It is widely used is GIS systems (ArcGIS, QGIS, etc.), spatial databases (PostGIS, MySQL Spatial Extensions, etc.), and various libraries (GDAL, GEOS, etc.). This talk is an introduction to Simple Features for R (the sf package) in the context of the Tidyverse framework.
name: GIS in Tidyverse, an introduction to the R sf package
